title: Implement sudo re-exec (detect EACCES, re-run with sudo)
description:
Re-execute with sudo on permission error.

## Story Context
- **Story:** Apply Mechanism
- **Purpose:** Users can apply their ~/.hosts changes to /etc/hosts safely
- **Contributes To:** Exit state #4 (apply mechanism works)

## Requirements
Add to src/core/apply.ts:
- Detect EACCES error when writing /etc/hosts
- Re-exec current process with sudo
- Pass through all original arguments
- Preserve stdin/stdout/stderr
- Exit with sudo process exit code

Use Bun.spawn() for re-exec:
```typescript
const result = Bun.spawn(['sudo', Bun.argv[0], ...Bun.argv.slice(1)], {
  stdio: ['inherit', 'inherit', 'inherit']
});
await result.exited;
process.exit(result.exitCode);
```

## Files
- src/core/apply.ts (modify)

## Verification
- Detects EACCES correctly
- Re-execs with sudo
- Preserves arguments
- User is prompted for password once
- Works in interactive and non-interactive contexts
close_reason: Completed: sudo re-exec mechanism implemented with EACCES detection, atomic writes, and root check

title: Resolve Review P1: malformed-marker rejection only end-to-end-tested for 2 of 5 shapes
description:
## Problem Statement

Phase 1B (.65) added strict extractManagedBlock that rejects 5 malformed-marker shapes: multiple BEGINs, multiple ENDs, BEGIN without END, END without BEGIN, END-before-BEGIN. Unit tests at tests/core/apply.test.ts:88-158 assert all 5 throw at the extractor. But only 2 (BEGIN-only and double-BEGIN) are asserted at the applyHostsFile level to produce zero writes (tests/core/apply.test.ts:178-225). The remaining 3 (END-only, nested, END-before-BEGIN) have no end-to-end no-write assertion. If a future refactor catches the throw inside buildNewContent or applyHostsFile and recovers, three corruption paths regress silently — defeating the whole-phase safety contract.

## Evidence

**File:** tests/core/apply.test.ts
**Lines:** 178-225 (only 2 cases at applyHostsFile layer)

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] test.each table
Parameterize with test.each(MALFORMED_FIXTURES) so each of the 5 shapes asserts: (1) applyHostsFile rejects, (2) renameSync was never called, (3) writeFileSync was never called.
**Effort:** Small (~10 lines)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 5 malformed shapes asserted at applyHostsFile level
- [ ] Each case asserts no fs.renameSync AND no fs.writeFileSync call

title: Resolve Review P1: apply.ts dedup refactor drops disabled entries from managed-block output
description:
## Problem Statement

The Phase 1B dedup refactor (hosts-cli-379.66, commit a7b4267) changed renderGroupBlockLines in src/core/apply.ts to filter group.entries by e.enabled, dropping disabled entries entirely from the managed-block output. Design.md is explicit (D-data-model line 108 and rendered example at lines 140-147) that 'enabled: false → emitted as a # -commented line'. This is a silent user-visible regression: disabled entries vanish from /etc/hosts on the next apply, and the textual toggle semantics are broken.

## Evidence

**File:** src/core/apply.ts (post-refactor renderGroupBlockLines)
**Design:** docs/hostie/design.md:108, :140-147

The new renderGroupBlockLines filters with .filter(e => e.enabled) before emitting lines. The pre-refactor code (and the canonical render.ts:renderEntry) emits enabled entries as '<ip> <hostname>' and disabled entries as '# <ip> <hostname>'.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Delegate to render.ts:renderEntry
Stop maintaining a local renderer in apply.ts. Iterate all entries (no enabled filter), call renderEntry from render.ts which already encodes the enabled/disabled rule. Emit the '# group: <path>' header for any group with at least one entry. Restores single-source-of-truth (also addresses P2 finding C — dry-run vs apply renderer divergence).
**Effort:** Small

### Option B — Inline fix only
Remove the .filter(e => e.enabled) in renderGroupBlockLines; emit the commented form locally. Keeps the two renderers split (doesn't address C).
**Effort:** Trivial

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New regression test in tests/core/apply.test.ts uses the rendered example from design.md:140-147 as fixture; round-trip parse → apply → /etc/hosts content matches expected commented disabled entries
- [ ] dry-run output equals apply-written output for the same input (couples this fix with finding C)
- [ ] Existing apply/render tests still pass
- [ ] No new test failures in full bun test run
close_reason: Fixed: render.ts:renderEntry now prefixes disabled entries with '# ' per design.md:108; renderHostsFile + apply.ts:renderGroupBlockLines iterate all entries (no enabled filter); design.md:140-147 fixture regression test added; old 'filters out disabled' assertions inverted to assert presence of commented form

title: Resolve UAT P1: sudo re-exec broken in compiled binary (uses Bun.argv[0] virtual path)
description:
## Problem Statement

Found in Phase 3 UAT against D10. When hostie is compiled with bun build --compile, Bun.argv[0] is '/$bunfs/root/hostie' — Bun's embedded virtual-filesystem entrypoint, not a real on-disk path. src/core/apply.ts:59 calls Bun.spawn(['sudo', Bun.argv[0], ...Bun.argv.slice(1)]). sudo cannot exec a virtual path and fails immediately with: 'error: Module not found "/$bunfs/root/hostie"'.

This completely defeats D10 (sudo re-exec on EACCES). Every non-root user attempting hostie apply hits this. No apply path works in production.

## Evidence

**File:** src/core/apply.ts:52-65
**Reproducer:** ./dist/hostie apply (as non-root, after ./dist/hostie add 10.0.0.42 uat-test.local)
**Observed:** 'error: Module not found "/$bunfs/root/hostie"'
**Expected:** sudo prompt → /etc/hosts written

## Why the test suite missed it
tests/core/reexec.test.ts asserts against Bun.argv[0] directly (same broken value), so mocked spawn captures show 'expected' argv but production sudo cannot use it. Confirms review finding N (P2 review-p2-reexec-integration): reexec tests are mock-shape only and lack an end-to-end EACCES integration test. Confirms review finding V (P3 review-p3-realpath-argv): argv[0] handling was already flagged as suspect.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A — [Recommended] Use process.execPath
src/core/apply.ts:59 — replace Bun.argv[0] with process.execPath. process.execPath is the real on-disk path to the running binary (works for bun-compiled binaries, bun run, and node). One-line change.

```ts
const result = Bun.spawn(["sudo", process.execPath, ...Bun.argv.slice(1)], {
  stdio: ["inherit", "inherit", "inherit"],
});
```

### Option B — process.execPath + realpathSync
A then resolve symlinks once: const argv0 = realpathSync(process.execPath). Combines this fix with P3 .V hardening. Recommended belt-and-suspenders.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/core/apply.ts:reexecWithSudo uses process.execPath (resolved via realpathSync) instead of Bun.argv[0]
- [ ] Update tests/core/reexec.test.ts to assert spawn argv[1] is process.execPath (or its realpath)
- [ ] Add NEW integration test: simulate EACCES from renameSync inside writeEtcHosts, assert Bun.spawn is called AND the captured argv[1] is process.execPath. (This closes review-p2-reexec-integration too — link in close reason.)
- [ ] Manual UAT: ./dist/hostie apply as non-root → sudo prompt → /etc/hosts updated → exit 0
- [ ] No regression in existing reexec or apply tests
close_reason: Fixed: src/core/apply.ts:reexecWithSudo uses realpathSync(process.execPath) instead of Bun.argv[0]; existing reexec tests updated; new integration test asserts EACCES from writeEtcHosts triggers sudo with resolved execPath (also closes review-p2-reexec-integration-uzc)

title: Resolve Review P1: clamp /etc/hosts mode to drop other-write before rename
description:
UAT D12 finding (2026-05-25): after `./dist/hostie apply`, /etc/hosts is 0646 (world-writable). Two scenarios both demand the fix:

(A) File was already 0646 pre-apply: writeEtcHosts faithfully preserved it via lstat+chmod. Preserving world-write on /etc/hosts is unsafe \u2014 any local user can DNS-hijack.
(B) File was 0644 pre-apply: regression introduced by writeEtcHosts (mask too permissive, or stat-followed-symlink-target).

Either way: writeEtcHosts must clamp /etc/hosts to a safe mode before rename.

Implementation:
- src/core/etchosts.ts:writeEtcHosts \u2014 after lstat, compute `safeMode = (stats.mode & 0o0777) & ~0o022` (drop group-write and other-write), or hard-code 0o644 for /etc/hosts specifically.
- Generalizes P3 finding U (mode mask 0o7777 preserves setuid/setgid/sticky) \u2014 close U as resolved by this fix.
- Tests: tests/core/apply.test.ts perm-preservation \u2014 add case: stat returns 0o100646 \u2192 chmod called with 0o644 (or 0o0644 & ~0o022 = 0o0644). Add case: stat returns 0o102755 (setuid) \u2192 chmod called with 0o0755 & ~0o022 = 0o0755.

Acceptance: `./dist/hostie apply` followed by `ls -l /etc/hosts` shows -rw-r--r-- (0644). Bun test passes.

Epic: hosts-cli-379
close_reason: Fixed: writeEtcHosts now masks (stats.mode & 0o0777) & ~0o022 — strips other-write, group-write, setuid/setgid/sticky. Added 2 tests (0o100646→0o644, 0o106755→0o755). Closes P3 finding U.

title: Resolve Review P1: TUI keybindings for add/edit/new-group (D9 acceptance)
description:
UAT D9 finding (2026-05-25): TUI launches and navigates, but there is no way to add an entry, edit an entry, or create a group from inside the TUI. Design D9 explicitly requires 'Full CRUD + search/filter inside the TUI \u2014 ...inline-edit IP/hostname/aliases, add new entries, add new groups...'. design.md:243 + 254 + 261 specify keybindings: [a] add, [e] edit, [d] delete (already wired), [Space] toggle (already wired).

The modals exist and are fully implemented (EntryEditorModal, GroupCreatorModal) and ModalHost in App.tsx already wires entry-creator, entry-editor, group-creator modal types. Only the keybindings in src/tui/hooks/useKeyboard.ts are missing.

Implementation:
- Add 'a' \u2192 openModal('entry-creator')
- Add 'e' \u2192 if selectedEntryId, openModal('entry-editor', { entry: <looked-up entry> })
- Add 'g' \u2192 openModal('group-creator', { parentPath: selectedGroupPath })
- Skip 'g a' two-key sequence for v1 (can be follow-up); single 'g' is simpler.

Tests:
- tests/tui/app.integration.test.tsx: add 3 tests \u2014 'a' opens entry-creator modal; 'e' on a selected entry opens entry-editor modal with that entry's data; 'g' opens group-creator modal.

Acceptance:
- ./dist/hostie \u2192 press 'a' \u2192 entry editor appears \u2192 Esc closes.
- Select an entry with j/k \u2192 press 'e' \u2192 editor populates with that entry's IP/hostname.
- Press 'g' \u2192 group creator appears.

Epic: hosts-cli-379
close_reason: Fixed: wired 'a'/'e'/'g' keybindings in useKeyboard.ts to existing entry-creator/entry-editor/group-creator modals. Updated help hint. 4 integration tests added.

title: Resolve Review P1: TUI search wiring (D19/D9 acceptance)
description:
UAT D19 finding (2026-05-25): TUI has no '/' search keybinding and no SearchBar UI. Hook (src/tui/hooks/useSearch.ts) and store (searchQuery, mode='search', setSearchQuery) exist but are never called from App.tsx or useKeyboard.ts. Design D9 requires 'fuzzy-search across entries and groups (with filter-by-group)'.

Implementation:
- useKeyboard.ts: '/' → setMode('search') + setSearchQuery(''); when mode==='search', character input appends to query, Backspace pops, Esc exits search mode + clears query, Enter exits search mode preserving query.
- New src/tui/components/SearchBar.tsx renders below GroupTree/EntryList when mode==='search' or searchQuery !== ''.
- App.tsx: call useSearch() to derive filteredEntries; pass to EntryList when searchQuery non-empty.
- Update helpHint to include '/ search'.

Tests:
- '/' enters search mode
- typed chars accumulate in searchQuery
- Esc clears query and exits mode
- Enter exits mode but preserves query
- filteredEntries reflect query

Epic: hosts-cli-379
close_reason: Fixed: wired '/' search keybinding in useKeyboard, search-mode input handling (chars accumulate, Backspace pops, Esc clears+exits, Enter exits preserving query). App.tsx now calls useSearch() and passes filtered results to EntryList when active. StatusBar shows '/query' with live match count. Help hint updated. 5 integration tests added.

title: Resolve Review P2: apply --check-perms / always-clamp mode even on no-op
description:
UAT D12 follow-up (2026-05-25): when applyHostsFile short-circuits via idempotency check (newContent === currentContent), it never re-stats or re-chmods /etc/hosts. A file that is content-correct but mode-wrong (e.g. someone did chmod 646 /etc/hosts out-of-band) stays mode-wrong forever \u2014 the .73 clamp only fires on writes.

Options:
(a) Always lstat /etc/hosts before the no-op return; if mode differs from clamped target, run a chmod-only path (no rename needed since we're not changing content).
(b) Add an explicit `apply --check-perms` flag that forces the perms reconciliation even when content matches.
(c) Document the limitation: 'hostie apply only fixes perms when it writes; run a manual chmod or touch + apply to reconcile.'

Recommended: (a) \u2014 silent perms reconciliation matches the spirit of D12 (atomic+correct) without requiring a new flag.

Acceptance:
- sudo chmod 646 /etc/hosts; ./dist/hostie apply (no content changes); ls -l /etc/hosts \u2192 0644.
- Test: stat returns 0o100646, no content diff \u2192 expect chmodSync called on /etc/hosts directly with 0o644, no rename, no writeFileSync.

Source: UAT D12 follow-up, post hosts-cli-379.73.

title: Resolve Review P2: writeEtcHosts uses statSync (follows symlinks) — use lstatSync
description:
Epic: hosts-cli-379

## Problem
src/core/etchosts.ts:writeEtcHosts captures mode/uid/gid via statSync, which follows symlinks. If /etc/hosts is a symlink to e.g. /etc/shadow (legitimate hardened-distro pattern or attacker-installed), perms captured belong to the target. Rename replaces the symlink with a regular file carrying wrong perms (0640 root:shadow → unprivileged DNS broken; minor info-disclosure vector).
## Fix
Use lstatSync. If symlink detected, EITHER refuse to write (recommended) OR fall back to 0o644 / no-chown defaults.
## Acceptance
- [ ] writeEtcHosts uses lstatSync
- [ ] Symlinked target path tested (mock fs.lstatSync → isSymbolicLink true)
- [ ] Documented behavior: refuse or fallback

title: Resolve Review P2: temp dir in os.tmpdir() risks EXDEV cross-filesystem rename failure on Linux
description:
Epic: hosts-cli-379

## Problem
src/core/etchosts.ts:writeEtcHosts creates the temp file via mkdtempSync(tmpdir() + 'hostie-'). On Linux where /tmp is commonly tmpfs (or a different partition from /etc), renameSync throws EXDEV after the strict-marker check passes — confusing low-level errno never exercised by mocked tests.
## Fix
Create temp dir in path.dirname(ETC_HOSTS_PATH) (e.g. /etc/.hostie-*). mkdtempSync gives 0700; safe in /etc. Guarantees same-filesystem atomic rename.
## Acceptance
- [ ] mkdtempSync target is dirname(ETC_HOSTS_PATH)
- [ ] Test covers cross-fs scenario or documents that real /etc writes are now atomic
- [ ] No regression in existing apply tests
